questions.question: What is the maximum ratio of Airmen to FAC augmentees when conducting components of the PFA?
options: 10 Airmen for every one FAC augmentee
12 Airmen for every one FAC augmentee
15 Airmen for every one FAC augmentee
8 Airmen for every one FAC augmentee

questions.question: What minimum fitness assessment score must a Physical Training Leader (PTL) maintain?
Which form is mandatory for documenting fitness assessment results, and locally-produced scorecards cannot be used?
What method does the Body Composition Program (BCP) use to calculate body composition?
According to the BCA assessment standards, what WHtR value is the threshold between meeting standard and not meeting standard?
When must Air Force military members complete their annual Body Composition Assessment (BCA)?
options: Within the first quarter of the fiscal year
Within the month designated by their unit commander

questions.question: All components of the fitness assessment must be completed within what time window on the same day?
What is the correct order of fitness assessment components?
options: Cardiorespiratory, muscular strength, core endurance, body composition
Muscular strength, core endurance, body composition, cardiorespiratory
Body composition, core endurance, muscular strength, cardiorespiratory
Body composition, muscular strength, core endurance, cardiorespiratory

questions.question: How many maximum points can be earned for the aerobic component of the fitness assessment composite score?
What composite score is required to achieve a fitness assessment level of 'Excellent'?
If an Airman receives an 'Unsatisfactory' fitness assessment result, within how many days must they retest?
What is the minimum rest period required between fitness assessment components?
What is the general recommendation for carbohydrate intake as a percentage of total daily caloric intake?
How many calories per gram do fats provide?
What is the optimal composition of a post-recovery beverage or snack in terms of carbohydrate to protein ratio?
What is the recommended daily sodium intake limit for adults according to the Dietary Guidelines for Americans?
Tobacco use is estimated to cost the Department of Defense how much annually due to increased healthcare utilization and decreased work productivity?
What is the minimum distance that designated tobacco use areas must be from building entrances on USAF installations?
What does the ADAPT program acronym stand for?
options: Alcohol and Drug Assessment Prevention Training
Alcohol and Drug Abuse Prevention and Treatment
Airman Drug and Alcohol Prevention and Testing
Assessment and Deterrence of Alcohol Problems and Treatment

questions.question: What are the four categories in which stress reactions typically appear?
What does the ACE model in suicide prevention stand for?
What are the four domains of Comprehensive Airman Fitness (CAF)?
According to the chapter, consuming large doses of caffeine (roughly 400-500 milligrams at one time) can result in what condition?
What is the recommended total fiber intake per day to aid in digestion and help balance the absorption of sugar into the bloodstream?
For an Airman who achieves an 'Excellent' fitness assessment with all three applicable component scores at or above 90, how often must they retest?
What training requirements must be completed before an Airman can be certified as a Physical Training Leader (PTL)?
options: Annual physical exam and commander's endorsement
Completion of an online fitness certification course only
Hands-on Basic Life Support training (or equivalent), CPR/AED training, and fitness assessment procedures
A passing score of 90 or higher on the fitness assessment

questions.question: What is the Body Composition Program (BCP) described as?
options: A voluntary wellness initiative for overweight Airmen
A medical screening program conducted by healthcare providers
A physical fitness test replacement program
A unit commander driven program that replaces the abdominal circumference measurement

questions.question: What is the composite score range for a 'Satisfactory' fitness assessment result?
How often must an Airman with a 'Satisfactory' fitness assessment result retest?
What is the duration of a recommended dynamic warm-up before vigorous physical activity?
What is the Principle of Recovery in fitness training?
options: Allow at least a 48-hour recovery period between workouts for the same muscle groups
Allow at least a 24-hour recovery period between all workouts
Allow at least a 72-hour recovery period between any type of exercise
Recovery is only necessary after cardiorespiratory workouts

questions.question: According to AFH 1, what is the Principle of Regularity in fitness?
options: Exercise once per week for maximum results
Exercise daily without rest days for fastest improvement
Exercise routinely to produce a training effect, with a routine of three workouts per week being best for optimal gains
Exercise only when preparing for a fitness assessment

questions.question: How many calories per gram do carbohydrates and proteins each provide?
What is the general recommendation for protein intake as a percentage of total daily calories?
What is the general recommendation for fat intake as a percentage of total daily calories?
According to AFH 1, the body is made up of about what percent water?
Dehydration occurs with fluid losses of greater than what percentage of body weight?
Saturated fats should make up less than what percentage of daily calories?
What does the acronym SMART stand for when setting nutrition and fitness goals?
options: Specific, Measurable, Attainable, Relevant, and Timely

questions.question: According to AFH 1, to avoid over-hydration, fluids should be limited to no more than how many cups per hour and per day?
options: 4 cups per hour and 36 cups per day
6 cups per hour and 48 cups per day
8 cups per hour and 64 cups per day
10 cups per hour and 80 cups per day
